Calculation.Text: SetProperty ( "level" ; 1 ) & SetProperty ( "server_mode" ; "Preview" ) & SetProperty ( "package_dir" ; FAT Package::Export Package Sub Path ) & SetProperty ( "package_friendly_id" ; FAT Package::JSON Friendly id ) & SetProperty ( "package_type_name" ; FAT Package::Package type name ) & SetProperty ( "fat_version" ; FAT Package::FAT version number )
"View all media with subtitles in title"
"View all text styles defined in title"
"Enter a description of the usage of this package. It's only for authoring and development purposes."

StyledText.Data: The referene name is used in internally in FAT. NOTE! The reference name is used in URL:s - if you link to any content in this package you need to export and publish this package again.
Is a Funkis Cloud launch package

If True = 1, this package is one of the packages that can be launched by the end-user from Funkis Cloud. This is only used by FAT internal to make it easier to copy links to the packages that the end-users will launch. I.e Title site

Calculation.Text: "Enter name of package directory. Leave blank to use title directory if only having one package"

StyledText.Data: The package directory is where all content in this package will be exported - json files and media files. A package can be seen as standalone app and the package directory gives the complete package publish path. For some older Titles, there is only one package, then it’s possible to leave this field empty.

The backend determines which functionality will be available on the server and affects mainly the use of IOs. Please note, Funkis Authoring Tool will not hide features that cannot be used on Web-server, LMS or xAPI backend. LMS IO data will be saved in suspend_data according to SCORM-standard. IO data cannot be shared between packages. xAPI In development. Funkis Cloud All IO features can be used. All users become members in a group and in a session where all input data is stored and can be shared between packages within the same Title.

Calculation.Text: "Check building block compatibility and completion status."

StyledText.Data: Import texts from other Funkis tools
Keep all texts in a Package stored in FAT. Import texts from Funkis Designer Tool. Don’t use this function for importing subtitles or SprigStory.

For some Learning Programmes, some texts may have been created outside the Funkis CMT, i.e. from SprigStory or Funkis Designer Tool. These external texts needs to be imported to Funkis CMT to keep all texts in a learning programme intact. In the box below, you’ll find the necessary functions to import and update these external texts, before sending them to translation. All imported texts will be referenced with their source file, which will be used when updating and deleting these texts only. For subtitle text documents, please see

Calculation.Text: SetProperty ( "caller" ; "Package setting" ) & SetProperty ( "title_id" ; FAT Title::PrimaryKey ) & SetProperty ( "package_id" ; FAT Package::PrimaryKey ) & SetProperty ( "localisation_code" ; FAT Localisation @ Package::Localisation code ) & SetProperty ( "localisation_id" ; FAT Localisation @ Package::PrimaryKey ) & SetProperty ( "localisation_stage" ; FAT Localisation @ Package::Localisation stage )
Not IsEmpty (FAT Localisation @ Package::Title localisation id)
SetProperty ( "localisation_id" ; FAT Localisation @ Package::PrimaryKey ) & SetProperty ( "translation_method" ; FAT Localisation @ Package::Translation method)
IsEmpty(FAT Localisation @ Package::Stage enabled localisation code)
IsEmpty(FAT Localisation @ Package::Stage enabled localisation code)

StyledText.Data: Select a language to add to the translation list. Select a method for translation. The method will decide the following steps.

Calculation.Text: IsEmpty(FAT Title::gSelectedLocalisation) Or IsEmpty ( FAT Title::gTranslationMethod )

StyledText.Data: This is the file suffix all original text-json files will be exported with.
Note: Languages in translation stage lower than 4 will not be included in export .

Calculation.Text: SetProperty ( "title_id" ; FAT Package::Parent id) & SetProperty ( "package_id" ; FAT Package::PrimaryKey ) & SetProperty ( "localisation_code" ; FAT Title::Original localisation code)
"Arrange text for export in contextual order."

StyledText.Data: Package text was arranged in contextual order: <<TextInContextualOrderTimeStamp>>.
Below you'll find some special tools for managing odd situations within the localisation process
If the original texts have been updated in a review - this function will set all localised texts to status = "Not reviewed".
Set edited texts to Not reviewed

ScriptReference.name: Set localised texts to not approved

Calculation.Text: ValueCount( FAT Modifier @ Package::ValuesList) > 4 Or FAT Modifier @ Package::Type ≠ "Value list radio"
ValueCount( FAT Modifier @ Package::ValuesList) < 5 Or FAT Modifier @ Package::Type ≠ "Value list radio"
Not PatternCount ( FAT Modifier @ Package::Type ; "Value list")

LayoutObject.name: String Number Value

Calculation.Text: FAT Modifier @ Package::Type = "Media" Or FAT Modifier @ Package::Type = "Color" Or FAT Modifier @ Package::Type = "Text"

LayoutObject.name: Color FM
Color Value
TextStyle Value
TextStyle Editor
Text TRANS

Calculation.Text: FAT Modifier @ Package::Type ≠ "Text" Or FAT Title::gCurrentLocalisationCode = FAT Title::Original localisation code Or IsEmpty ( FAT Title::gCurrentLocalisationCode)

LayoutObject.name: Text

Calculation.Text: FAT Modifier @ Package::Type ≠ "Text" Or Not IsEmpty ( FAT Title::gCurrentLocalisationCode) And FAT Title::gCurrentLocalisationCode≠FAT Title::Original localisation code
FAT Modifier @ Package::Type ≠ "Media" Or Not SubtitleMedia ( Media MV PK::Container file extension )

LayoutObject.name: Edit media
Link

StyledText.Data: The shared code base for this title is: <<Base Project @ Title::Name>>

Calculation.Text: "If a stateToggle or Attribute is missing - press this button"
FAT Export Log @ Package::Package id = FAT Package::PrimaryKey /*and PatternCount ( Remote Export Log @ Package::Export Mode Explained ; "content" )*/
PatternCount ( FAT Export Log @ Package::Export Mode Explained ; "code" )
PatternCount ( FAT Export Log @ Package::Export Mode Explained ; "code" )
PatternCount ( FAT Export Log @ Package::Export Mode Explained ; "code" )
PatternCount ( FAT Export Log @ Package::Export Mode Explained ; "code" )
PatternCount ( FAT Export Log @ Package::Export Mode Explained ; "code" )

StyledText.Data: The package directory is where all content in this package will be exported - json files and media files. A package can be seen as standalone app and the package directory gives the complete package publish path. For some older Titles, there is only one package, then it’s possible to leave this field empty.
JSON files only, except Text Text and JSON files Updated media only, Text and JSON All media, Text, JSON, Font CSS, Fonts, List media

CharacterStyle.Text: Updated media only, Text and JSON
All media, Text, JSON, Font CSS, Fonts, List media

StyledText.Data: There are several options for file export from Funkis CMT: Local Files are exported to a local folder on the user’s hard drive. This option is only available for developers. Remote Files are sent to the choosen remote server, using the Title id as an identiifier. When exporting remote please allow time for file transfer when refreshing browser. JSON All JSON files - cmt_page.json, cmt_page-item.json, cmt_tag.json and cmt_symbol.json are exported to the JSON folder. Text All text files, including subtitles and imprted texts are exported - in all languages. Updated media Only media files that have been changed since last publish will be exported. This is the only available option for authors and editors. All media All media files are exported. Export to Word All text content in the package is exported to a Word-file on the desktop.

ParagraphStyle.Text: All media files are exported. Export to Word

StyledText.Data: Full path: <<Publish Server Package Full Path>>
Export package files using settings above

Package specific setting - Funkis Player If you want to override the Title Funkis Player for this package, select a Funkis Player for this package. You will get an email when the preview link is updated. Title settings: <<Baseproject Codebase @ Title::Name>>

CharacterStyle.Text: If you want to override the Title Funkis Player for this package, select a Funkis Player for this package. You will get an email when the preview link is updated.

StyledText.Data: You can select a code base for the remote host during testing. You have now selected the code base: <<Baseproject Codebase @ Package::Short name>> <<Baseproject Codebase @ Package::Description>> After selecting code base, you need to publish your choice to the remote. This can take several minutes and you will be notified via email when the project on the remote host is updated.

Calculation.Text: Not PatternCount ( Baseproject Codebase @ Package::Short name ; "version" )

StyledText.Data: If the export don't include some text or media. This script reset text and media fields to this package.

Calculation.Text: "Check building block compatibility and completion status."

StyledText.Data: Check building block compatibility and completion status.

Calculation.Text: "If header or subheader text fields are unavailable, the text might be re-created. "

StyledText.Data: If header or subheader text fields are unavailable, the text might be re-created.
Deletes the package and all content within. This operation cannot be undone.
Some useful tools for managing the package

Calculation.Text: SetProperty ( "clone_level" ; "Package" ) & SetProperty ( "item_id" ; FAT Package::PrimaryKey ) & SetProperty ( "item_parent_id" ; FAT Package::Parent id ) & SetProperty ( "item_description" ; FAT Package::Reference name ) & SetProperty ( "item_sortindex" ; FAT Package::SortIndex )

StyledText.Data: Duplicates the package and all content within. This operation cannot be undone.
Duplicates the package and moves it to a new title. This operation cannot be undone.

Calculation.Text: IsEmpty ( FAT Package::GitLab Project code URL )
"The project specific URL to GitLab for this package. Leave empty for base project."

StyledText.Data: URL to the GitLab project for this package. Leave blank if no Project exist on GitLab yet. Delivery and Preview will then use Funkis Base project. When we receive an approval of the Master version a Project on GitLab must always be created.
Download package for delivery You will be notified with an email including download links when your package is built and ready for download.

CharacterStyle.Text: You will be notified with an email including download links when your package is built and ready for download.

Calculation.Text: FAT Package::Codebase id = "681B4BEC-A556-40C6-9CA2-3C5C08C53EFC" And IsEmpty(FAT Package::GitLab Project code URL) Or IsEmpty ( FAT Package::Delivery format ) Or FAT Package::Package Backend = "Funkis Cloud"
FAT Export Log @ Package::Package id = FAT Package::PrimaryKey /*and PatternCount ( Remote Export Log @ Package::Export Mode Explained ; "content" )*/

StyledText.Data: Check the delivery formats you want to create and download. Please note, for Quiddity and XAPI-productions,SCORM and WEB_LINK will not deliver full functionality.

Calculation.Text: Not PatternCount ( Baseproject Codebase @ Package::Short name ; "version" )

LayoutObject.name: test22

StyledText.Data: Log from task server. NOTE: deliveries are temporary and entries older than 2 weeks will be automatically deleted. SCORM Cloud may still have older history

You can select a code base for the remote host during testing. You have now selected the code base: <<Baseproject Codebase @ Package::Short name>> <<Baseproject Codebase @ Package::Description>> After selecting code base, you need to publish your choice to the remote. This can take several minutes and you will be notified via email when the project on the remote host is updated.

Calculation.Text: "Check building block compatibility and completion status."
IsEmpty ( Title Environment::Publish Server id) And FAT Package::Package Backend = "Funkis Cloud"

StyledText.Data: Basically all text entries in Funkis CMT can be limited in number of characters. The reason is to ensure a good-looking end result. Check with the art director or the developer of the learning programme to learn the number of max characters for titels and subtitles for the navigation-items Learning programem, Course, Chater and Page. When a user is exceeding the amount of characters, a red warning tag will be visible. It is possible to enter more characters than defined here. This function is especially valuable for localisations.

Tags (or labels) can be used for categorising specific content. Each learning programme has it’s own unique set of tags. Tags can be used for many different purposes, such as job roles (”technician”, “manager”, etc) or difficulty level (”Easy”, “Medium”, “Hard”). The functionality for the tag is defined by the program code. Tags can be set on Course-level, Chapter-level, Page-level or Page item-level. Check the boxes where tags applies for this learning programme.

Clear storage When publishing remote and previewing the content, use this setting to decide if the package should clear storage for each new publish. True: Publishing new content clears local storage which means that each test starts from a new, clean state with no previous user data. False: Publishing new content does not clear local storage. If there are any user data - like IO input - it will be stored even if content has been updated.
Import subtitles created by Funkis Designer Tool

FAT can import and update subtitles for multiple media files from a Funkis Designer generated subtitle JSON file. Import subtitles New subtitles will be created in FAT when importing. A warning will be triggered if importing subtitles that already exist in FAT. Please note, Subtitle texts that cannot be matched with an exisitng media file in the CMT will NOT be imported! Update subtitles Existing subtitles will be updated with new text and enter and exit times from the selected Funkis Designer subtitle file. New subtitles will be created in FAT for media files that havent’t been subtitled previously. Media with subtitles - Deleting subtitles needs to be done on a media by media basis. - Importing VTT subtitles needs to be done on a media by media basis.

CharacterStyle.Text: FAT can import and update subtitles for multiple media files from a Funkis Designer generated subtitle JSON file.

New subtitles will be created in FAT when importing. A warning will be triggered if importing subtitles that already exist in FAT. Please note, Subtitle texts that cannot be matched with an exisitng media file in the CMT will NOT be imported!

Existing subtitles will be updated with new text and enter and exit times from the selected Funkis Designer subtitle file. New subtitles will be created in FAT for media files that havent’t been subtitled previously.
needs to be done on a media by media basis.
needs to be done on a media by media basis.

ParagraphStyle.Text: FAT can import and update subtitles for multiple media files from a Funkis Designer generated subtitle JSON file. Import subtitles New subtitles will be created in FAT when importing. A warning will be triggered if importing subtitles that already exist in FAT. Please note, Subtitle texts that cannot be matched with an exisitng media file in the CMT will NOT be imported! Update subtitles Existing subtitles will be updated with new text and enter and exit times from the selected Funkis Designer subtitle file. New subtitles will be created in FAT for media files that havent’t been subtitled previously. Media with subtitles - Deleting subtitles needs to be done on a media by media basis.

StyledText.Data: <<FAT Theme TextStyle::Custom Name>> Style type: <<FAT Theme TextStyle::Type>>

LayoutObject.name: TextStyle name Copy4
TextStyle FontFamily
